skill.name: brand-guidelines-anthropic
skill.description: Applies Anthropic-inspired colors, typography, and visual styling guidance to artifacts that need a consistent Anthropic look and feel.
skill.summary: Style documents and presentations with Anthropic-inspired brand colors and typography.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged license URLs, Markdown backticks, and keyword matches as possible risks. Review found only documentation, color values, font guidance, and Apache License text; no executable code, network behavior, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License URLs Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are standard Apache License reference links in LICENSE.txt. They are not fetched, executed, or used by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are part of static license text. No code path or instruction uses these URLs for network access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: License Text Is Not Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts match ordinary Apache License legal wording. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic implementation appears at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are plain legal prose from Apache License 2.0. There is no executable cryptographic API usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Color Values Are Not Commands
low_findings.description: The backtick alerts in SKILL.md wrap color hex values for Markdown formatting. They are not Ruby backtick execution or shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks contain static hex color literals in Markdown bullets. SKILL.md is documentation and contains no Ruby code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Documentation Keywords Are Not Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The remaining alerts point to descriptive text about brand styling, fonts, and RGBColor. No system or network reconnaissance instructions are present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines describe brand keywords, fallback fonts, and color application. I found no commands that inspect systems, ports, networks, or credentials.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent visual styling when creating Anthropic-related artifacts. This skill provides practical color and typography guidance for presentations, documents, and visual assets.
actual_capabilities: Lists Anthropic-inspired primary and accent colors with exact hex values.
Recommends Poppins for headings and Lora for body text.
Defines fallback fonts for systems without custom fonts.
Explains how to apply colors to text, backgrounds, and shapes.
Supports brand-style review for documents, slides, and visual artifacts.
limitations: Does not include official logo files or downloadable brand assets.
Does not install fonts or verify local font availability.
Does not automatically edit files without a host tool workflow.
Does not grant trademark usage rights or legal approval.

use_cases.title: Style a Partner Presentation
use_cases.description: Apply consistent colors, heading fonts, body fonts, and accent shapes to a slide deck.

use_cases.title: Review a Product Brief
use_cases.description: Check whether a document uses appropriate Anthropic-inspired colors, typography, and visual hierarchy.

use_cases.title: Prepare Design Handoff Notes
use_cases.description: Create concise brand styling instructions for designers or developers implementing a visual artifact.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Styling Request
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a simple document or slide deck.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply Anthropic-inspired brand colors and typography to this artifact. Keep the layout readable and preserve the existing content structure.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Review
prompt_templates.scenario: You need feedback before sharing an artifact.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this artifact for Anthropic-inspired brand consistency. Identify color, typography, hierarchy, and spacing changes needed.

prompt_templates.title: Presentation Refresh
prompt_templates.scenario: You need a polished slide deck style.
prompt_templates.prompt: Refresh this presentation with Anthropic-inspired styling. Use appropriate heading fonts, body fonts, background colors, and accent colors.

prompt_templates.title: Implementation Guidance
prompt_templates.scenario: You need detailed design instructions for another tool or teammate.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create implementation notes for applying Anthropic-inspired brand styling to this artifact. Include colors, typography, shape treatment, and limitations.

output_examples.input: A slide deck uses mixed fonts and bright unrelated colors.
output_examples.output: The deck uses Poppins-style headings, Lora-style body text, dark primary text, light backgrounds, and restrained orange, blue, and green accents.

output_examples.input: A report needs a quick brand consistency review.
output: Use the dark primary color for main headings.
Use the light background color for content sections.
Reserve accent colors for callouts, dividers, and supporting shapes.

output_examples.input: A designer needs handoff guidance for a branded one-page artifact.
output_examples.output: Use Poppins-style headings, Lora-style body copy, high contrast text, and a limited accent palette for secondary visual elements.

best_practices: Start with existing content hierarchy before changing colors or fonts.
Use accent colors sparingly for emphasis, not for every element.
Check contrast and readability after applying the palette.
anti_patterns: Do not treat the skill as legal approval for trademark usage.
Do not replace all visual elements with accent colors.
Do not assume custom fonts are installed on every system.

faq.question: Does this skill include official Anthropic assets?
faq.answer: No. It provides styling guidance only. It does not include logos, templates, or downloadable brand files.

faq.question: Can it edit slide decks directly?
faq.answer: Only when the host environment provides suitable document or presentation editing tools.

faq.question: Which fonts does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends Poppins for headings and Lora for body text, with Arial and Georgia fallbacks.

faq.question: Is this an official Anthropic skill?
faq.answer: No. The report identifies it as a community source.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No evidence of required network access was found during review.

faq.question: Can it guarantee brand compliance?
faq.answer: No. It can guide visual consistency, but final brand and legal review remains the user's responsibility.
